papers.title: Scattering cross sections of solid ortho-deuterium for ultracold neutrons: Role of defects, phonons, and temperature
papers.authors: Stefan Döge, Jürgen Hingerl, and Christoph Morkel
papers.summary: Author(s): Stefan Döge, Jürgen Hingerl, and Christoph MorkelSolid ortho-deuterium is a widely used converter material in ultracold-neutron (UCN) sources. Although it has been studied with neutrons for more than two decades, some of its scattering properties remain poorly understood until this day. We remeasured the total scattering cross section of solid deu…[Phys. Rev. C 113, 044614] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026

papers.title: Nonflow suppression in flow analysis with a maximum likelihood estimator
papers.authors: Chong Ye, Wei-Liang Qian, Cesar A. Bernardes, Sandra S. Padula, Rui-Hong Yue, Yutao Xing, and Takeshi Kodama
papers.summary: Author(s): Chong Ye, Wei-Liang Qian, Cesar A. Bernardes, Sandra S. Padula, Rui-Hong Yue, Yutao Xing, and Takeshi KodamaWe show that the maximum likelihood estimator (MLE) is an effective tool for mitigating nonflow effects in flow analysis. To this end, one constructs two toy models that simulate nonflow contributions corresponding to particle decay and momentum conservation, respectively. The performance of MLE is …[Phys. Rev. C 113, 044912] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026
